message:
feat(doctor,runtime): improve capture diagnostics and expand effect executor parity tests

Extend capture.rs with improved error handling. Expand effect_executor
parity test coverage (+39 lines). Sync beads tracker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1rz0.9","title":"Responsive Reflow — E2E PTY Resize Storm Tests (Verbose Logs)","description":"PTY tests that hammer resize events and verify no flicker/tearing artifacts; log full traces.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Responsive Reflow — E2E PTY Resize Storm Tests (Verbose Logs)
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
- Logging: E2E logs are JSONL with stable schema (run_id, case, env, seed, timings, checksums, capabilities, outcome)

Tests exercise full render pipeline (Buffer→Diff→Presenter→HeadlessTerm) under burst, sweep, oscillate, pathological, and mixed storm patterns with verbose JSONL logging, flicker analysis integration, and performance budget enforcement.","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:26:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1rzr","title":"Action Timeline: Alert Threshold Calibration (Conformal + e-process)","description":"## Goal
Set change-point alert thresholds with formal false-positive control and provide an explanation ledger per alert.

## Core Idea (Alien Artifact)
Conformal thresholding on residuals from the sketch baseline:
- Given calibration residuals R = {r_1..r_n}, threshold q = quantile_{1-alpha} using (n+1) rule.
- Alert when r_t > q.

Anytime-valid layer:
- Maintain an e-process on residuals to allow early alerts without inflating false positives.
- Example: e_t = exp(lambda * (r_t - mu) - (lambda^2 * sigma^2)/2), E_t = product e_t.
- Trigger if E_t > 1/alpha.

## Acceptance Criteria
- FPR <= alpha on synthetic and recorded traces.
- Alert carries evidence ledger: residual, q, conformal score, e-value, alpha.
- No alert spam under steady-state traffic.

## Tests (REQUIRED)
- unit_conformal_threshold: threshold equals (1-alpha) quantile of calibration set.
- unit_evalue_monotone: e-process nonnegative and grows with evidence.
- property_fpr_control: empirical FPR <= alpha + tolerance.

## E2E (REQUIRED)
- Replay trace with known change points; log alert timing and evidence.

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.

 {"id":"bd-1sder","title":"task(asupersync-architecture): define invariants, user-visible metrics, and canonical evidence schema","description":"Background:
The migration needs explicit invariants and user-visible success metrics. Otherwise validation becomes vague and benchmarks measure the wrong things.

Goal:
Define the invariant set, user/operator metrics, and evidence schema that every later task must trace back to.","design":"Work packages:
- Enumerate correctness invariants: semantic parity, deterministic output, shutdown guarantees, terminal safety, replay identity.
- Enumerate user/operator metrics: effect turnaround, shutdown latency, child-process cancellation latency, failure diagnosability, doctor completion time, artifact completeness.
- Define the canonical evidence schema and correlation IDs that tests and scripts must emit.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Every later test and rollout task can point to named invariants and metrics from this bead.
2. Metrics are user-meaningful and operator-meaningful, not only internal counters.
3.  {"id":"bd-1sm6v","title":"epic(alien-artifact): Galaxy-Brain Transparency Demo Screen","description":"Live UI showing: (1) posterior distributions for all active decisions, (2) top evidence terms with Bayes factors, (3) chosen actions with expected loss, (4) confidence intervals. Force drift then show automatic fallback. The 'killer demo' from FrankenSuite summary. This is what makes FrankenTUI feel like an alien artifact — the math is visible and beautiful.

**User Value:** Interactive galaxy-brain cards let users see exactly why each adaptive decision was made, building trust in the framework.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Decision card widget renders at all 4 disclosure levels
- Live sparkline shows posterior probability evolution
- Demo screen works in both inline and alt-screen modes
- All existing tests pass","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:50:12.751206044Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:40.109226266Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:40.109203323Z","close_reason":"Merged into bd-1lgz8 (existing Killer Demo Galaxy-Brain Transparency epic)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","demo","galaxy-brain"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1sm6v","depends_on_id":"bd-fp38v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1un","title":"Document Terminal Compatibility Matrix","description":"# Terminal Compatibility Matrix Documentation

## Background
Plan Chapter 16 provides a detailed compatibility matrix. This needs to be documented for users to understand what features work where.

## Compatibility Matrix (from Plan)

| Feature | Kitty | WezTerm | Alacritty | Ghostty | iTerm2 | GNOME Term | Win Term |
|---------|-------|---------|-----------|---------|--------|------------|----------|
| True Color | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Sync Output | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |
| OSC 8 Links | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✗ |
| Kitty Keyboard | ✓ | ✓ | ✗ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |
| Kitty Graphics | ✓ | ✓ | ✗ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |
| Sixel | ✗ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ |
| Focus Events | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Bracketed Paste | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |

## Feature Details

### True Color (24-bit RGB)
- Detection: COLORTERM=truecolor or COLORTERM=24bit
- Fallback: 256-color or 16-color palette
- Most modern terminals support this

### Synchronized Output (DEC 2026)
- Purpose: Atomic frame updates, flicker prevention
- Detection: Known terminal identification
- Fallback: Buffered writes still help
- ftui activates when detected

### OSC 8 Hyperlinks
- Purpose: Clickable links in terminal
- Format: `\x1b]8;;URL\x1b\\text\x1b]8;;\x1b\\`
- Windows Terminal: Not supported
- Most Linux/macOS terminals support

### Kitty Keyboard Protocol
- Purpose: Enhanced key event information
- Provides: press/repeat/release, modifiers
- Limited adoption outside Kitty ecosystem
- Feature-gated in ftui

### Scroll Region (DECSTBM)
- Purpose: Inline mode optimization
- Support: Nearly universal
- Quirks: tmux/screen passthrough issues
- ftui uses as optimization, not requirement

## Multiplexer Notes

### tmux
- Passthrough: Requires `\x1bPtmux;...\x1b\\`
- Sync output: Blocked by default
- OSC 8: Needs recent version + config
- Detection: TMUX env var

### screen
- Limited modern feature support
- No sync output passthrough
- Basic ANSI works fine
- Detection: STY env var

### Zellij
- Better passthrough than tmux/screen
- Native terminal support varies
- Detection: ZELLIJ env var

## Windows Considerations

### Windows Terminal
- True color: ✓
- Mouse: ✓
- OSC 8: ✗
- Sync output: ✗
- Raw mode: Via Crossterm

### ConEmu/Cmder
- Limited feature set
- True color: ✓ (newer versions)
- ANSI support varies by version

### cmd.exe / PowerShell (legacy)
- Very limited ANSI support
- Windows 10+ improved support
- Use Windows Terminal for best experience

## Inline Mode Notes
- Works everywhere (no special requirements)
- Cursor save/restore: Universal
- Scroll region: Nearly universal (optimization only)
- Sync output: Nice-to-have (not required)

## Recommended Test Matrix
For CI, test against:
1. Kitty (most features)
2. WezTerm (cross-platform)
3. Alacritty (popular, minimal)
4. Plain Linux VT (baseline)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Matrix table documented
- [ ] Feature descriptions complete
- [ ] Multiplexer notes included
- [ ] Windows section clear
- [ ] Inline mode notes present
- [ ] Test matrix recommended

 {"id":"bd-1vb18","title":"Fix emoji-triggered border corruption in demo showcase (keep emojis)","description":"Problem
- Demo showcase has border misalignment/corruption in File Browser and Markdown screens when emojis are present. Emojis must remain.

Likely Causes
1) Several screens try to "clear" the full area using Paragraph::new("") + bg style, but Paragraph only paints style and does not clear cell content. With Flex gaps between panels, stale border characters from prior frames/screens can bleed into those gaps and appear as misaligned borders.
2) Buffer::print_text_clipped (ftui-render) advances by grapheme_width() but may only write the first char for multi-codepoint graphemes (e.g. VS16 sequences like "⚙️"), leaving holes that can preserve stale border chars.

Fix
- Clear demo screen root areas with actual space cells (bg=surface) so gaps are deterministically blank.
- Make Buffer::print_text_clipped fill any extra width when falling back to first char so no holes remain (and add a regression test).

Acceptance
- File Browser and Markdown screens render without stray/misaligned borders in gaps.
- Emojis remain enabled in file browser icons and unicode showcase.
- cargo fmt/check/clippy/test pass.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"GrayFalcon","created_at":"2026-02-06T02:01:09.704771751Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T02:34:21.706728130Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T02:34:21.706701631Z","close_reason":"Completed (emoji border corruption fixed: VS16 width policy + buffer filler + empty paragraph clears)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["borders","demo-showcase","emoji","render","text"]}
